channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump anti-sanctuary city executive order could target federal funding, says expert
item.description: President Donald Trump hinted at signing an executive order limiting sanctuary cities, but such an order will likely face legal challenges along the way, an expert tells Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch live: Senate holds hearing on rising antisemitism on college campuses
item.description: The Senate Health, Education, Labor and Pensions (HELP) Committee is holding a hearing on the perceived rise of antisemitism on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Walz announces remote work changes for Minnesota state agency employees
item.description: State agency employees will have to work in-person for at least 50 percent of scheduled workdays starting June 1. The policy change provides an exemption for employees who live more than 75 miles away from their primary work location.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: A lawyer for a Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University says she has been detained by Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Canada PM calls Trump tariffs a 'direct attack;' Trump downplays Signal exposure
item.description: The United Auto Workers leader is praising President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on vehicles and parts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he 'always thought' Waltz was responsible for Signal chat scandal
item.description: President Donald Trump told reporters on Wednesday that he "always thought" national security advisor Mike Waltz was responsible for the Signal chat scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-abortion groups mount effort to strip Planned Parenthood funding ahead of Supreme Court hearing
item.description: Major anti-abortion groups are gathering in the nation's capital to begin a lobbying effort with Congress and President Donald Trump’s administration aimed at eliminating funding for Planned Parenthood.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says Signal chat isn't 'really an FBI thing.' The FBI has a long history of such inquiries
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel may not have been part of a Signal chat in which other Trump administration national security officials discussed detailed attack plans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump nominates Georgia state Sen Brandon Beach for US treasurer
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday nominated Georgia state Sen. Brandon Beach for U.S. treasurer. He has served as a state senator in Georgia since 2013.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal judge denies Trump admin's effort to ban transgender people from military
item.description: A federal judge denied the Department of Defense's effort to block transgender people from enlisting in the U.S. military under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congressional Democrats find their voice as Signal slip-up becomes talk of the town
item.description: Democrats took the opportunity to drill into CIA Director John Ratcliffe and Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard during "worldwide threats" hearings this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats take hope from upset win in a GOP-leaning Pennsylvania state Senate district
item.description: MANHEIM, Pa. (AP) — Democrat James Andrew Malone narrowly won a special election for a Pennsylvania state Senate seat in Republican-leaning suburbs and farming communities, scoring an upset for a party roiled by infighting since President Donald Trump’s victory.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge says Justice Department attacked her character to 'impugn the integrity' of US judicial system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge accused the Justice Department on Wednesday of attacking her character in an effort to undermine the integrity of the judicial system, forcefully pushing back against the Trump administration's criticism of the courts for rulings that blocked parts of the president'
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration backtracks on some ID requirements after backlash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration is partially backtracking on a plan that would require all new and existing beneficiaries to travel to a Social Security field office to verify their identity.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congressional Republicans target PBS, NPR funding in contentious hearing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A House Republican pushing the Trump administration's government efficiency efforts called for dismantling and defunding the nation's public broadcasting system following a contentious hearing Wednesday featuring the heads of PBS and NPR.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration potential travel ban concerning airlines: Editor
item.description: "Even the announcement that they're being proposed on the list is sending a chill through the travel industry.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Florida House proposes largest sales tax cut in state history
item.description: The Florida House of Representatives is proposing the largest cut to sales tax in state history, from 6% to 5.25%.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Republican: White House ‘in denial’ about Signal war plans chat
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.) said the White House is “in denial” over The Atlantic’s explosive report about a text chain with senior-level administration officials — and, inadvertently, a reporter — that discussed plans for a U.S. attack on the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements for buying the kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayoral poll: Cuomo grows lead, Mamdani gains traction
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is still holding onto the lead in the New York City mayoral race – but one other candidate is also gaining support, according to new exclusive polling conducted by PIX11 News in partnership with Emerson College and The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: The White House says nothing classified was shared on Signal. Democrats say that strains credulity
item.description: Trump administration officials are struggling to stem the fallout from revelations that top national security officials discussed sensitive attack plans over a messaging app and mistakenly added a journalist to the chain.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Stephen Groves, And David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NRA legislative expert says gun rights could see 'most monumental' win in Congress since 2005
item.description: Two major gun rights bills are currently advancing their way through Congress, including legislation seeking to create nationwide reciprocity for concealed carry permits, something the NRA says would be the "most monumental" win for Second Amendment rights in over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s new executive order could upend voting
item.description: The order tests the power of Trump’s authority and would require voters using a federal form to show proof of citizenship to register to vote in federal elections. It’s sure to be tested in court.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Republicans look to DOGE model for state government efficiency commission
item.description: The commission would consist of a panel of six citizens who would investigate fraud in state agencies and potential conflicts of interest in the Legislature. Three members would be appointed by Republicans and three by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Republican lawmakers seek to put PBS and NPR in the hot seat
item.description: A House subcommittee led by Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene and named after Elon Musk’s government-efficiency team has set its sights on the public broadcasters.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: In Jamaica, Marco Rubio vows to revisit US travel warning
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the State Department will re-evaluate its travel warnings for Jamaica and other countries to ensure they reflect real conditions on the ground.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate confirms Michael Kratsios to lead White House science, tech office
item.description: Kratsios, who was Trump’s chief technology officer during his first White House term, was approved Tuesday by the upper chamber in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Trump's nominee to run NIH
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed health researcher Jay Bhattacharya as the next leader of the National Institutes of Health (NIH). Bhattacharya was confirmed on a party- line vote, 53 to 47.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: DHS suspends green card processing for refugees, asylees
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said it is pausing review of green card applications for some refugees and asylees, leaving in limbo those who came to the U.S. after fleeing unrest. The directive from U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: The inside story of how a journalist was sent White House war plans
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, the editor of The Atlantic, said he was mistakenly added to a group chat with U.S. national security leaders about imminent military strikes on Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan, Ailsa Chang, Christopher Intagliata, and Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump defends Waltz after Signal breach
item.description: President Trump said he still has confidence in national security adviser Mike Waltz in an interview with NBC on Tuesday, following a report by a journalist for The Atlantic who says he was included in a text message chain with officials about plans for an attack on Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: US infrastructure improved with Biden-era spending but there's a long way to go
item.description: A report card on the upkeep of America's infrastructure gave it a “C” grade on Tuesday, up slightly from previous years.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's tariffs meant to punish Venezuela, former ambassador says
item.description: Trump has announced countries buying oil or gas from Venezuela will face a 25% "secondary tariff" starting April 2.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation
